Summary: Baca County Commissioners provide rationale for and state “Be it resolved that: 1-the Western Slope of Colorado is a community of interest that deserves to be made whole  within one congressional district. 2- the Eastern Plains and Southern Colorado is a community of interest that deserves its own congressional district. And 3- we encourage the Congressional Redistricting Commission to ensure Colorado's rural population is effectively represented through the creation of two fully rural (or as near as possible) districts.”
"Crowley County would support having two rural districts in Colorado."  Attached resolution.

Full Comment:
I think otero  and crowley counties should be together and otero be kept whole.

Western slope should remain whole. 

Eastern colorado is more ag and energy.

COI: Agriculture, Education, Public Health, Trades areas (tourism), Water needs and supplies, Other (veterans issues)
Summary: Rural Colorado shares the crucial common interests of reliance on river water and our agricultural economy, with other shared interests: are transportation, education, veterans issues, rural hospitals, tourism and the park system. These are unique to our east and west regions and have little in common with the urban corridor. Please preserve the representation of the rural east and west regions of Colorado.
Full Comment:
Dear Members of the Congressional Redistricting Committee,

As a business and a ranch owner in Pueblo and Otero Counties, my hope is that you will preserve the representation of the rural east and west regions of Colorado. Rural Colorado shares the crucial common interests of reliance on river water and our agricultural economy. Other shared interests are transportation, education, veterans issues, rural hospitals, tourism and the park system. These issues are unique to Colorado's east and west regions. The needs of rural Colorado have little in common with the urban corridor and bedroom communities to the north. Central Colorado, the I-25 corridor from Fountain to Fort Collins, should have its own representation. Please keep these issues in mind as you plan..
Respectfully,
Trish Leone

COI: Rural, Agriculture, Environment,Industry, Water needs and supplies
Summary: In the population considered rural (1/4 state population), each region and each county faces a unique set of challenges around agricultural practices and sustainability, energy production, wide reaching water issues, and natural resource management, and more. Supports joint plan of Pro 15, Action 22, and Club 20.
Full Comment:
I am a citizen that support two separate counties (Weld and Logan counties) through varying boards. As this discussion pertains to redrawing the lines of congressional districts I have a few things that I think should be considered. First and foremost, 1/4th of this states population live in what would be considered rural or non urban communities of interest. With that being said, each individual region faces wide ranging issues such as agricultural practices and sustainability, energy production, wide reaching water issues, and natural resource management to name a few. This brings me to my second point. None of these 55 counties face the exact same issues and quite often are issues far afield from those faced in urban centers. With that being said I believe the joint plan, drafted by Pro 15, Action 22, and Club 20, will provide the most suitable and balanced representation for Coloradoans as a whole. By having two distinct rural districts the issues faced by these communities of interest can be addressed more succinctly. This would also allow for more concise direction on energy and agriculture, which the eastern half of the state is so well known for. 
Thank you, 
Kreg Vollmer

Summary: Supports two full rural districts as part of our new eight congressional districts. Rural areas, such as ours in Yuma County, need full representation in Washington DC. . Our economy is driven by agriculture production and energy which the more populated areas do not deal with.
Full Comment: I am writing to support two full rural districts as part of our new eight congressional districts. Rural areas, such as ours in Yuma County, need full representation in Washington DC that will support our interests and listen to our issues. Rural residents and businesses are unlike those in urban and suburban areas of our state. Counties on the Eastern Plains have nothing in common with our urban neighbors such as Fort Collins and Castle Rock. Our economy is driven by agriculture production and energy which the more populated areas do not deal with. Without proper representation for even just our economic needs, we will not be able to stand firmly for our way of life that we all know and love. The Eastern Plains and the Western Slope have both been historically recognized as communities of interest, in which both should be taken seriously with people that will continue that path to what makes Colorado so special. Colorado is not just a huge city. The majority of our state is rural America, which is why making two large rural districts would make the most sense and then compact the other six within our urban areas. Please consider these points when redistricting our congressional seats which were originally made to represent all of America in all states and the different regions within those states.

Summary: Counties on the eastern Plains have little in common with the front range (Fort Collins to Colorado Springs). The eastern Plains have historically been recognized as a community of interest as has been the Western Slope. Make two large rural and six more compact districts.
Full Comment: I feel that the counties on the eastern Plains have little in common with the front range counties from Fort Collins to Colorado Springs. The eastern Plains have historically been recognized as a community of interest. The Western Slope is historically recognized as a community of interest. Making two large rural districts allows for the other six to be compact. Please give thought and consider to this worthy request.

Summary: Comment mentions a proposal to make Eastern Colorado and Western Colorado each their own Congressional districts. Commenter is very much in favor of this proposal.
Full Comment:
there is a proposal to make Eastern Colorado and Western Colorado each their own Congressional districts.  I am very much in favor of this proposal.  As a life long resident of Eastern Colorado we would like to have a representative that really understands and appreciates our way of life in the rural areas.  We are very much a community of interest, with Agriculture and energy our main economic drivers, in each of the two mentioned districts. Please give very thoughtful consideration to this proposal.
Thank you
Steve Young

Summary: Action22 Board of Directors has adamantly supported Amendments Y&Z since 2018. What matters most to our communities is relevant, reliable representation. This year, the Action22 Board agreed, following the directives set out in Y&Z, the best way to accomplish that would be two purely rural districts. Action22, Club 20 and Pro 15 Executive Directors and Boards have had lengthy discussions and are all in agreement on this issue. Our rural communities need and deserve the full representation two rural districts would provide.
Full Comment: Please see letter and map attachment.

Summary: Rural Colorado needs representation. Two rural districts as proposed by other groups should be considered
Unnamed: 19: 1-Yes (.pdf - not found but same as Club20, Pro15, Action 22)
Full Comment: Considering the two rural districts gives us a better voice and representation as presented by Club 20, Pro15, and Action 22's map attached. It is my opinion that rural Colorado needs to be represented well in the future of CO.  Please deeply consider the life blood of Colorado which is represented by the rural regions.

Summary: Pro 15, an advocacy group representing interests in the northeastern part of Colorado would like to submit a map for your consideration. This would designate two rural districts with communities of interest leaving the other six districts to come from the front range from Colorado Springs north.
Full Comment:
Dear Commission, Pro 15, an advocacy group representing interests in the northeastern part of Colorado would like to submit a map for your consideration.  This would designate two rural districts with communities of interest leaving the other six districts to come from the front range from Colorado Springs north.
This map would make the 4th CD also include some of the Southeast and San Luis Valley area including Pueblo moving them from the 3rd CD.  The configuration shown is based on the population numbers we have at this point.   Leaving the 3rd CD to encompass the Western Slope.
No configuration is going to be completely competitive because the puzzle pieces just will not fit.  The area for the Congressional District 4 has a large community of interest in energy (both oil and gas and renewables) agriculture and entrepreneurship.  We are also Republicans, Democrats, and Unaffiliated and we need an undiluted voice for our interests.
In this area, we have the top 9 agriculture-producing counties in the state along with 2 of the top oil and gas counties.  The renewable future of the state, including wind and solar, is among the fastest-growing industries.  Agriculture has expanded to hemp production, dairies, value-added agriculture industries that continue to increase farm efficiency and it provides many options for help with carbon capture and the protection of the environment.
We also have lots of miles between towns and people and have different transportation, health care, education, business, and water needs, to name a few.  We require representation that is familiar with these issues and is willing to be the voice for our region.
This region has a deep-rooted pride in family and historical heritage as well as being known as problem solvers and entrepreneurs.
We are the major conduit for delivery of goods and services with our two Interstates and several east/west state highways and a north/south route being developed from Mexico to Canada that goes through the eastern part of the state.
Along with Action 22 and Club 20, we will be sending a request to testify in person before the commission to provide further information and background.  Between the 54 counties shown we represent Â¼ of the stateâ€™s population.
Thank you,
Cathy Shull
Executive Director
Pro 15
Fort Morgan, CO 
970-768-2230     Cathy@Pro15.org

Summary: Wants 2 rural districts so as to not be overlooked by metro areas
Full Comment: I like the idea of 2 rural districts so my rural voice is heard and not diluted by the metro area. So many times rural areas are overlooked and not heard because of the numbers in the metro areas.

Summary: Create two rural districts of Western Slope & Eastern Plains. Weld sould be included in these. Vote diluted when with urban area
Full Comment: I am writing to request that the commission create two fully rural districts from our now 8 seats in Congress. Agriculture is one of the predominant industries in Colorado and deserves to have representation as such.  These two districts should consist of the western slope and the eastern plains, including Weld County. These areas are communities of interest, due to their focus on agriculture and energy production, and each have been traditionally recognized as such. Our voice is diluted when agricultural areas are combined with urban/suburban areas, since these two entities have extremely different ways of looking at questions of regulation, taxation, land use, or commerce.

Summary: Letter urges Commission to be critical and thoughtful when reviewing the “Two Rural Districts” proposal. Commentor cautions the Commission on oversimplifying “Rural.” (Example: Larimer, Mesa, Pueblo, and Weld Counties each have at least one major population center that is large enough (50,000 people or more) to be classified by the U.S. Census Bureau as urban, not rural). While a lot of areas may have “agricultural” industries and a “rural” history they are seeign a population explosion and experienceing some of the fastest population growth in the state.  These areas are not "rural;" they’re no longer small towns or disconnected from city issues. “They have all the opportunities –and challenges –of growing urban centers.” Letter concludes that Colorado’s congressional districts should reflect these changed circumstances rather than be framed solely because of antedated labels like “urban” and “rural.”
Supports map that has two rural districts and not grouped with urban areas.
Full Comment: I support the redistributing map that show two distinct rural districts. Our rural communities must have representation of rural interest and not be grouped in with the more populated counties such as Douglas County.

Summary: Supports the creation of Western Slope and Eastern Plains CDs due to COIs in the regions
Full Comment: I appreciate the work that has gone into the creation of the preliminary map. Maintaining the integrity of the 3rd and 4th Congressional Districts as shared communities of interest will greatly benefit both agriculture and energy production. These industries are vital to Colorado and I do hope the commission will not make any substantial changes to this map.

Summary: Rural Colorado faces distinct challenges. Advocates use of map proposed by Club 20.
Full Comment:
Hello,
I would like to reiterate point made in a previous public commentary I made on this topic.  Rural Colorado faces distinct challenges as a community of interest.  Challenges can be better addressed with people who have geographic ties.  For this reason I would advocate for the map proposed by Club 20 and ask you to sincerely consider the letter and map sent by the Executive Director, Christian Reece.  

Thank you, 
Jen Schumann

Thank you for serving on this commission. I understand that youâ€™ve given up a large part of your summer to serve. You are appreciated!
 
I want you to know that I very much like the Congressional discussion map that you have drawn.

It is common sense, in my opinion, to keep the Western slope of Colorado separate from the Eastern slope. Many of us moved from the cities to enjoy the rural lifestyle.
Additionally, our forests, mountains, agriculture, farming and ranching, tourism are for the most part, here on the west slope and weâ€™d like to not divide these entities. An example of my point; the vote for the introduction of the gray wolves to the West that the Metro areas gave us. The West clearly voted against the wolves issue. Itâ€™s important that our vote not be polluted even more.

My basic request to you is to not dilute urban vs rural. 
Please keep the West slope whole!

Thank You,
Donna Russo

Summary: "I support the Club 20 maps. CD3 should be the entire Western Slope of Colorado, Unless absolutely necessary House and Senate Districts should not divide a County"
Full Comment:
I support the Club 20 maps.
 CD3 should be the entire Western Slope of Colorado
 Unless absolutely necessary House and Senate Districts should not divide a County
 Thank you for the chance to testify.
 K. J. Heinschel
